     3	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     4	// MODULE: Coverage Graph Session Helpers
     5	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     6	// Shared session normalization and filtering helpers for the
     7	// in-memory coverage-graph CJS libraries.
     8	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     9	
    10	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
    11	   1. NORMALIZATION
    12	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    13	
    14	/**
    15	 * Normalize a session identifier into a comparable string.
    16	 * @param {unknown} sessionId
    17	 * @returns {string|null}
    18	 */
    19	function normalizeSessionId(sessionId) {
    20	  if (typeof sessionId !== 'string') return null;
    21	  const normalized = sessionId.trim();
    22	  return normalized.length > 0 ? normalized : null;
    23	}
    24	
    25	/**
    26	 * Read a normalized session identifier from a node record.
    27	 * @param {unknown} node
    28	 * @returns {string|null}
    29	 */
    30	function getNodeSessionId(node) {
    31	  if (!node || typeof node !== 'object') return null;
    32	  if (normalizeSessionId(node.sessionId)) return normalizeSessionId(node.sessionId);
    33	  if (node.metadata && typeof node.metadata === 'object') {
    34	    return normalizeSessionId(node.metadata.sessionId);
    35	  }
    36	  return null;
    37	}
    38	
    39	/**
    40	 * Read a normalized session identifier from an edge or its endpoint nodes.
    41	 * @param {{ nodes?: Map<string, object> }} graph
    42	 * @param {unknown} edge
    43	 * @returns {string|null}
    44	 */
    45	function getEdgeSessionId(graph, edge) {
    46	  if (!edge || typeof edge !== 'object') return null;
    47	  if (normalizeSessionId(edge.sessionId)) return normalizeSessionId(edge.sessionId);
    48	  if (edge.metadata && typeof edge.metadata === 'object') {
    49	    const metadataSessionId = normalizeSessionId(edge.metadata.sessionId);
    50	    if (metadataSessionId) return metadataSessionId;
    51	  }
    52	
    53	  const nodes = graph && graph.nodes instanceof Map ? graph.nodes : null;
    54	  if (!nodes) return null;
    55	
    56	  const sourceSessionId = getNodeSessionId(nodes.get(edge.source));
    57	  const targetSessionId = getNodeSessionId(nodes.get(edge.target));
    58	  if (sourceSessionId && (!targetSessionId || targetSessionId === sourceSessionId)) return sourceSessionId;
    59	  return targetSessionId;
    60	}
    61	
    62	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
    63	   2. FILTERING
    64	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    65	
    66	/**
    67	 * Check whether a node or edge belongs to the requested session.
    68	 * @param {{ nodes?: Map<string, object> }} graph
    69	 * @param {unknown} record
    70	 * @param {unknown} sessionId
    71	 * @param {'node'|'edge'} recordType
    72	 * @returns {boolean}
    73	 */
    74	function matchesSession(graph, record, sessionId, recordType) {
    75	  const requestedSessionId = normalizeSessionId(sessionId);
    76	  if (!requestedSessionId) return true;
    77	
    78	  const actualSessionId = recordType === 'edge'
    79	    ? getEdgeSessionId(graph, record)
    80	    : getNodeSessionId(record);
    81	
    82	  return actualSessionId === requestedSessionId;
    83	}
    84	
    85	/**
    86	 * Filter node identifiers by normalized session id.
    87	 * @param {{ nodes?: Map<string, object> }} graph
    88	 * @param {unknown} sessionId
    89	 * @returns {string[]}
    90	 */
    91	function getFilteredNodeIds(graph, sessionId) {
    92	  if (!graph || !(graph.nodes instanceof Map)) return [];
    93	  const results = [];
    94	  for (const [nodeId, node] of graph.nodes.entries()) {
    95	    if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(nodeId);
    96	  }
    97	  return results;
    98	}

   542	/**
   543	 * Extract the directory portion of a file path.
   544	 * @param {string} filePath
   545	 * @returns {string}
   546	 */
   547	function extractDirectory(filePath) {
   548	  if (!filePath) return '';
   549	  const lastSlash = filePath.lastIndexOf('/');
   550	  return lastSlash > 0 ? filePath.slice(0, lastSlash) : '.';
   551	}
   552	
   553	/**
   554	 * Compute hotspot spread: fraction of directories that contain files
   555	 * with above-median composite hotspot score.
   556	 * @param {Array<object>} files
   557	 * @returns {number}
   558	 */
   559	function computeHotspotSpread(files) {
   560	  if (!Array.isArray(files) || files.length === 0) return 0;
   561	
   562	  const allDirs = new Set();
   563	  const hotDirs = new Set();
   564	
   565	  const hotspotScores = files
   566	    .map((file) => computeFileHotspotScore(file))
   567	    .sort((a, b) => a - b);
   568	  const median = hotspotScores[Math.floor(hotspotScores.length / 2)] || 0;
   569	
   570	  for (const f of files) {
   571	    const dir = extractDirectory(f.path || '');
   572	    allDirs.add(dir);
   573	    const hotspotScore = computeFileHotspotScore(f);
   574	    if (hotspotScore >= median) {
   575	      hotDirs.add(dir);
   576	    }
   577	  }
   578	
   579	  return allDirs.size > 0 ? hotDirs.size / allDirs.size : 0;
   580	}
   581	
   582	/**
   583	 * Compute cluster diversity: fraction of unique clusters across domains.
   584	 * @param {Array<object>} domains
   585	 * @returns {number}
   586	 */
   587	function computeClusterDiversity(domains) {
   588	  if (!Array.isArray(domains) || domains.length === 0) return 0;
   589	
   590	  const clusters = new Set();
   591	  for (const d of domains) {
   592	    clusters.add(d.cluster || assignCluster(d));
   593	  }
   594	
   595	  return clusters.size / domains.length;
   596	}
   597	
   598	/**
   599	 * Assign a default cluster to a domain based on its name.
   600	 * Groups subdomains by registrable domain rather than raw TLD.
   601	 * @param {object} source
   602	 * @returns {string}
   603	 */
   604	function assignCluster(source) {
   605	  const domain = extractDomainHost(source);
   606	  if (!domain) return 'unclustered';
   607	  return computeRegistrableDomain(domain) || domain || 'unclustered';
   608	}
   609	
   610	/**
   611	 * Compute the composite hotspot score used for file ranking and spread.
   612	 * @param {object} file
   613	 * @returns {number}
   614	 */
   615	function computeFileHotspotScore(file) {
   616	  const complexity = typeof file.complexity === 'number' ? file.complexity : 1;
   617	  const churnRate = typeof file.churnRate === 'number' ? file.churnRate : 0;
   618	  const issueCount = typeof file.issueCount === 'number' ? file.issueCount : 0;
   619	  return (complexity * 0.4) + (churnRate * 0.35) + (issueCount * 0.25);
   620	}

   636	      return null;
   637	    }
   638	  }
   639	
   640	  return null;

   320	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),

   323	
   324	/**
   325	 * Transition the authoritative board status using the allowed-transition matrix.
   326	 *
   327	 * @param {object} board
   328	 * @param {string} targetStatus
   329	 * @returns {{ success: boolean, previousStatus: string, currentStatus: string, error?: string }}
   330	 */
   331	function transitionBoardStatus(board, targetStatus) {
   332	  if (!board || typeof board !== 'object') {
   333	    return { success: false, previousStatus: 'unknown', currentStatus: 'unknown', error: 'Invalid board' };
   334	  }
   335	
   336	  if (!BOARD_STATUSES.includes(targetStatus)) {
   337	    return {
   338	      success: false,
   339	      previousStatus: board.status || 'unknown',
   340	      currentStatus: board.status || 'unknown',
   341	      error: `Invalid target status: ${targetStatus}`,
   342	    };
   343	  }
   344	
   345	  const allowedStatuses = BOARD_STATUS_TRANSITIONS[board.status];
   346	  if (!Array.isArray(allowedStatuses)) {
   347	    return {
   348	      success: false,
   349	      previousStatus: board.status || 'unknown',
   350	      currentStatus: board.status || 'unknown',
   351	      error: `Invalid current status: ${board.status}`,
   352	    };
   353	  }
   354	
   355	  if (!allowedStatuses.includes(targetStatus)) {
   356	    return {
   357	      success: false,
   358	      previousStatus: board.status,
   359	      currentStatus: board.status,
   360	      error: `Cannot transition board from "${board.status}" to "${targetStatus}" (allowed next status(es): ${allowedStatuses.length > 0 ? allowedStatuses.join(', ') : 'none'})`,
   361	    };
   362	  }
   363	
   364	  const previousStatus = board.status;
   365	  board.status = targetStatus;
   366	  board.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   367	  return {
   368	    success: true,
   369	    previousStatus,
   370	    currentStatus: targetStatus,
   371	  };
   372	}

   375	   5. DASHBOARD RENDERING
   376	----------------------------------------------------------------*/

   379	 * Generate dashboard.md content from board.json.
   380	 * This is a derived render, never manually edited.

 succeeded in 0ms:

   505	  return (order[a] || 0) - (order[b] || 0);
   506	}
   507	
   508	/**
   509	 * Build the canonical 5-key merge-dedup key for a finding record.
   510	 * @param {object} findingRecord
   511	 * @returns {string}
   512	 */
   513	function buildFindingCompositeKey(findingRecord) {
   514	  return [
   515	    normalizeMergeKeyPart(findingRecord && findingRecord.sessionId),
   516	    normalizeMergeKeyPart(findingRecord && findingRecord.generation),
   517	    normalizeMergeKeyPart(findingRecord && findingRecord.segment),
   518	    normalizeMergeKeyPart(findingRecord && findingRecord.wave),
   519	    normalizeMergeKeyPart(findingRecord && findingRecord.findingId),

   521	}
   522	
   523	/**

   528	function buildFindingGroupKey(findingRecord) {

   532	    normalizeMergeKeyPart(findingRecord && findingRecord.findingId),
   533	  ].join('::');
   534	}

codex
**Findings**
1. `F-030-001` Severity `P1` Dimension `maintainability` File [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:96). Title: Board transition API exists, but the board still does not advance its own status canonically. `createBoard()` still hard-codes `status: 'initialized'`, `updateBoard()` only updates timestamps, segments, findings, and stats, and `transitionBoardStatus()` is only defined separately at [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:331). Recommendation: route normal lifecycle updates through `transitionBoardStatus()` or derive board status inside `updateBoard()`.

2. `F-030-002` Severity `P1` Dimension `maintainability` File [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:236), [wave-lifecycle.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-lifecycle.cjs:248), [wave-segment-state.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-state.cjs:185). Title: The merge contract still diverges across board, lifecycle, and segment-state helpers. The board now uses the 5-key composite key at [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:513), but lifecycle joining still keys by `findingId` or `file:line::title` at [wave-lifecycle.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-lifecycle.cjs:417), and segment-state merge still falls back to `file:line::title` at [wave-segment-state.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-state.cjs:370). Recommendation: promote one canonical merge-key contract and make the other two layers consume it.

3. `F-030-003` Severity `P2` Dimension `maintainability` File [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:146). Title: The old `computeMomentum` naming drift still survives as a public alias. The rename landed, but `computeMomentum` is still kept as `const computeMomentum = computeRecentEdgeActivity` at [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:170) and exported at [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:396). Recommendation: either deprecate and remove the alias after migration or document it as permanent public API.

4. `F-030-004` Severity `P2` Dimension `maintainability` File [coverage-graph-session.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-session.cjs:56). Title: Centralized session normalization introduced an undocumented target-node precedence rule. `getEdgeSessionId()` falls back from explicit edge session to source-node session, then silently to target-node session at lines 56-59. Recommendation: reject mixed-session edges without explicit `edge.sessionId`, or document and test this precedence rule explicitly.

5. `F-030-005` Severity `P2` Dimension `maintainability` File [session-isolation.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:371). Title: The collided-ID regression fixture still misses two higher-level read paths. The new fixture now covers `contradictions`, `status`, and `convergence` at [session-isolation.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:414), but there is still no collided-ID assertion for `coverage_gaps` or `unverified_claims` anywhere in that shared-ID block. Recommendation: extend the same fixture family to those two query types.

**Original Finding Status**
- `F-004-001` `FIXED` — session normalization is now centralized and auto-created nodes inherit session IDs at [coverage-graph-core.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-core.cjs:135) and [coverage-graph-session.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-session.cjs:19).
- `F-004-002` `PARTIAL` — mapped to `F-030-003`.
- `F-004-003` `FIXED` — cycle handling is now stabilized via SCC collapse at [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:250), with regression coverage at [coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:104).
- `F-008-001` `PARTIAL` — mapped to `F-030-001`.

**Findings**
1. `F-030-001` Severity `P1` Dimension `maintainability` File [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:96). Title: Board transition API exists, but the board still does not advance its own status canonically. `createBoard()` still hard-codes `status: 'initialized'`, `updateBoard()` only updates timestamps, segments, findings, and stats, and `transitionBoardStatus()` is only defined separately at [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:331). Recommendation: route normal lifecycle updates through `transitionBoardStatus()` or derive board status inside `updateBoard()`.

2. `F-030-002` Severity `P1` Dimension `maintainability` File [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:236), [wave-lifecycle.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-lifecycle.cjs:248), [wave-segment-state.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-state.cjs:185). Title: The merge contract still diverges across board, lifecycle, and segment-state helpers. The board now uses the 5-key composite key at [wave-coordination-board.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-coordination-board.cjs:513), but lifecycle joining still keys by `findingId` or `file:line::title` at [wave-lifecycle.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-lifecycle.cjs:417), and segment-state merge still falls back to `file:line::title` at [wave-segment-state.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-state.cjs:370). Recommendation: promote one canonical merge-key contract and make the other two layers consume it.

3. `F-030-003` Severity `P2` Dimension `maintainability` File [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:146). Title: The old `computeMomentum` naming drift still survives as a public alias. The rename landed, but `computeMomentum` is still kept as `const computeMomentum = computeRecentEdgeActivity` at [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:170) and exported at [coverage-graph-signals.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-signals.cjs:396). Recommendation: either deprecate and remove the alias after migration or document it as permanent public API.

4. `F-030-004` Severity `P2` Dimension `maintainability` File [coverage-graph-session.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-session.cjs:56). Title: Centralized session normalization introduced an undocumented target-node precedence rule. `getEdgeSessionId()` falls back from explicit edge session to source-node session, then silently to target-node session at lines 56-59. Recommendation: reject mixed-session edges without explicit `edge.sessionId`, or document and test this precedence rule explicitly.

5. `F-030-005` Severity `P2` Dimension `maintainability` File [session-isolation.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:371). Title: The collided-ID regression fixture still misses two higher-level read paths. The new fixture now covers `contradictions`, `status`, and `convergence` at [session-isolation.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:414), but there is still no collided-ID assertion for `coverage_gaps` or `unverified_claims` anywhere in that shared-ID block. Recommendation: extend the same fixture family to those two query types.
